Energy Secretary Says ‘No Guarantees’ Oil Prices Will Fall Soon
Energy Secretary Chris Wright said the Strait of Hormuz, a critical passageway for oil shipments, remained unsafe for tankers. Iran has been firing projectiles and laying mines.
Energy Secretary Chris Wright said on Sunday there were “no guarantees” that oil prices would fall in the coming weeks, despite President Trump’s having vowed repeatedly that the United States will try to force Iran to stop attacks on oil tankers in the Strait of Hormuz.
“There’s no guarantees in wars at all,” Mr. Wright said in an interview on ABC’s “This Week.” “I can guarantee the situation would be dramatically worse without this military operation to defang the Iranian regime.”
“Right now, our focus is destroying their military capabilities, including those that are used specifically to threaten the straits,” he added. “But we need to finish those tasks first, and you will see the straits open again in the not-too-distant future.”
title: Oil Prices May Not Fall Soon, U.S. Energy Secretary Says

citability.title: Oil Prices May Not Fall Soon, U.S. Energy Secretary Says
